headline: Washington forced Anthropic to pull its two most capable AI models worldwide, while an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day bled gigabytes of data from hundreds of organizations.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Weaponization
emerging_trends.text: Governments and criminals now treat frontier models as both controlled assets and attack tools—Washington pulled Anthropic's two best models the same day Google sued a Gemini-abusing phishing ring.

emerging_trends.subject: Long Dwell
emerging_trends.text: Decade-old footholds surfaced in tandem, as Velvet Ant hid inside Linux login software for nearly ten years and a phpBB authentication bypass sat unpatched for a decade.

emerging_trends.subject: Breach Penalties
emerging_trends.text: Regulators and courts extracted record sums for data losses, from South Korea's $409 million Coupang fine to the $47 million settlement fund approved for 23andMe's 7 million victims.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: Code-distribution channels keep turning into attack vectors, with more than 400 poisoned Arch packages and dark-web markets trading the GitHub access and API keys that seed the next compromise.

topics.title: Anthropic Pulls Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Under Export Order
topics.last_24h: Anthropic disabled Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for every user worldwide after the Trump administration ordered it at 5:21 p.m. ET to block all foreign nationals.
topics.summary: The U.S. government invoked export controls to bar foreign nationals, inside or outside the country, from Anthropic's two most advanced models, citing national security. Anthropic complied by suspending both globally yet disputed the basis, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the capability common elsewhere. The company frames Mythos 5 as an upgrade over Mythos Preview and Fable 5 as Mythos made safe for general use. Security professionals split over dual-use risk, safeguards, and tiered access.

sources.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story

topics.title: Google Sues China-Based Gemini Phishing Network
topics.last_24h: Google sued Outsider Enterprise, a China-based network it accuses of weaponizing its Gemini AI to run a phishing-as-a-service kit against Americans.
topics.summary: Google filed suit against the China-based cybercrime network behind a phishing-as-a-service kit called Outsider, which it says used the Gemini AI agent to craft scam text messages and websites. The operation has hit hundreds of thousands of victims, cost millions in losses, and links to more than 9,000 fake sites and a million fraudulent URLs. The case marks a push to hold AI-abusing fraud rings accountable in court.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
Google sues China-based scammers over Gemini AI abuse

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day Drains Hundreds of Organizations
topics.last_24h: Researchers disclosed an actively exploited zero-day in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP that has let attackers steal gigabytes of data from hundreds of organizations.
topics.summary: A critical flaw in Oracle-owned PeopleSoft software has exposed hundreds of organizations and hit American universities hardest. The extortion crew ShinyHunters seized on it to bulk-steal data across higher education. Administrators running PeopleSoft should patch immediately and hunt for signs of mass data export.

sources.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed

topics.title: 400-Plus Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked
topics.last_24h: Attackers hijacked more than 400 Arch User Repository packages and rewrote their build scripts to drop a Rust infostealer and an eBPF rootkit.
topics.summary: Attackers compromised over 400 packages this week in the Arch User Repository, Arch Linux's community-maintained collection. Any machine that built an affected package ran a Rust credential stealer aimed at developer secrets and access tokens; with root, the malware loads an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. Developers who built AUR packages recently should rotate secrets and inspect systems for the rootkit.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer

topics.title: Velvet Ant Backdoored Linux Login for Nearly a Decade
topics.last_24h: Sygnia detailed how the China-linked group Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM and OpenSSH components to hide inside a target network for close to ten years.
topics.summary: Velvet Ant, a China-nexus espionage group, planted its access inside the Linux login system—the PAM and OpenSSH components that authenticate every sign-in—where routine cleanup could not reach. The placement let the group persist for nearly a decade on the network it targeted. Defenders should audit authentication binaries and PAM modules for tampering rather than lean on endpoint tools alone.

sources.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: Section 702 Surveillance Authority Set to Lapse
topics.last_24h: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act will lapse for the first time since 2008 after Congress deadlocked on reauthorization.
topics.summary: Legislative gridlock has pushed Section 702 of FISA toward its first expiration since lawmakers passed it in 2008. The program lets U.S. agencies collect foreign intelligence and has long drawn privacy objections over incidental collection of Americans' communications. A lapse would interrupt a core surveillance authority until negotiators reach a deal.

sources.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock

topics.title: South Korea Fines Coupang $409 Million Over Breach
topics.last_24h: South Korea's data protection commission fined e-commerce company Coupang $409 million over a personal data breach, the largest such penalty it has ever issued.
topics.summary: South Korea's regulator levied a record $409 million fine against Coupang for a personal data breach, eclipsing the $88.8 million it fined SK Telecom earlier this year. The penalty signals aggressive enforcement on breach accountability. Companies operating in the country face steep exposure for lapses in personal data protection.

sources.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine

topics.title: Conti Operative Pleads Guilty in U.S. Court
topics.last_24h: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland pleaded guilty in the United States to conspiracy charges over his role in the Conti ransomware operation.
topics.summary: A Ukrainian man, extradited from Ireland to the U.S. last year, admitted conspiracy charges tied to Conti, one of the most prolific ransomware crews. The plea extends a slow run of arrests and prosecutions against the gang's members. It marks continued law-enforcement pressure on ransomware affiliates years after Conti's peak.

sources.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation

Marlins try to extend road win streak in matchup against

business_politics.text: The United States and Iran agreed on a final text to end the war that began February 28, with Pakistan mediating and negotiators expecting to sign within 24 hours; the accord reopens the Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly a fifth of the world's oil moves, and crude futures settled at multimonth lows.

sources.title: Pakistan says US-Iran deal to be signed within 24 hours

business_politics.text: SpaceX surged nearly a fifth on its trading debut after raising $75 billion in the largest initial public offering ever, valuing the rocket and AI company at $2.1 trillion and making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

sources.title: SpaceX’s surge on debut makes Musk world’s first trillionaire
SpaceX, Now Worth $2.1 Trillion, Pulls Off Goldilocks Debut

business_politics.text: US headline inflation reached 4.2% annually, the highest since April 2023, sharpening the Federal Reserve's choice between holding rates and acting.

sources.title: Chart of the Week: US headline inflation matters to consumers

business_politics.text: The Justice Department cleared Paramount's $81 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery, advancing David Ellison's drive to build a media empire; European regulators must still approve.

sources.title: Justice Department Clears Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery Deal

business.text: Fred Rogers Productions launched the first dedicated "Mister Rogers' Neighborhood" YouTube channel, streaming full episodes, livestreams, clips, and compilations worldwide for the first time.

sources.title: The first-ever dedicated Mister Rogers' Neighborhood YouTube channel launches globally

business.text: Anthropic took its newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, offline to comply with a Trump administration directive barring foreign nationals from using them on national security grounds.

sources.title: Anthropic says it has taken its latest AI models offline to comply with new export controls

business.text: Ford recalled more than 250,000 Focus cars whose engines can stall while driving, after repairs under an earlier recall went wrong.

sources.title: Ford recalls more than 250,000 Focus models because engines can stall unexpectedly

around_town.text: A Post-Gazette guide lays out where the candidates for Pennsylvania governor stand on abortion as the race takes shape.

sources.title: Where the candidates for Pennsylvania governor stand on abortion

around_town.text: Drivers parked along Ward Street in Oakland found their cars towed this week; residents say notice fell short, while officials say crews had approved the signage.

sources.title: Oakland residents say cars were towed without proper notice, officials say signage was approved

around_town.text: McKeesport school directors fired the entire staff of the high school football coach amid a controversy over his leadership dating to his 2016 hiring.

sources.title: McKeesport school directors fire football coach's staff amid controversy

around_town.text: The Peters Township spray park reopens this weekend after a malfunction forced a temporary closure.

sources.title: Peters Township spray park scheduled to reopen after malfunction caused closure

events.text: The Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival returns to Butler, the Jeep's birthplace, this weekend with a self-styled Jeep invasion.

sources.title: Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival marks Jeep's birthplace in Butler, Pa.

events.text: Pogopalooza, the world pogo-stick championships, bounces through Pittsburgh and Wilkinsburg this weekend after a downtown high-jump competition opened the event Friday.

sources.title: Pogopalooza bounces back to Pittsburgh this weekend

events.text: Kings & Queens of the Court returns to the courts on Barnes Street in New Kensington for the next several Saturdays, drawing area kids to play and sharpen their game.

sources.title: New Kensington excited as Kings & Queens of the Court makes its return

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is ending, with OpenAI and Anthropic racing to go public to secure exit liquidity for companies that burn billions a year and show no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Thompson examines Fable 5 as the public version of Mythos, judging it very capable while warning that it sets troubling new precedents around alignment and tiered AI access.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn’t AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport challenges the AI-as-industrial-automation analogy, questioning why predicted mass job displacement has not materialized and what that reveals about the technology's real limits.
